https://hg.mozilla.org/mozilla-central/r.commit_message:
Bug 1818026 - stop loading page style actor stuff for every process all the time, r=mconley,emilio

Differential Revision: https://phabricator.services.mozilla.com/D170498

ranked_scenarios.summary: Stop loading PageStyle actor for every content process

description.text:
Not that many pages use alternative page styles, but right now the actor loads everywhere, which is a little unfortunate both for process startup and for pages with tons of frames.

If we only start the child side of things when there is actually a `<link rel=stylesheet>` that has a `title` attribute, based on a custom event from DOM code (similar to bug 1745720 for link clicks), we can avoid this overhead.

I have a reasonably straightforward patch for this.
